title: Sangamo exits stage - Nature Biotechnology
text:
Access through your institution

Buy or subscribe

In June, Sangamo Therapeutics, a pioneer in genome editing, filed for bankruptcy while securing agreements with two pharma companies to maintain the value of some assets. Eli Lilly bid $50 million for the company’s AAV delivery system, its zinc finger and integrase technologies, and its prion program, whereas Astellas bid $25 million for the Fabry disease gene replacement therapy isaralgagene civaparvovec, the company’s most advanced clinical program. Sangamo launched in 1995 with what was then a groundbreaking technology and took multiple assets into clinical trials, but none advanced into pivotal trials, let alone approval.Sangamo was formed around a patent thicket for zinc finger nucleases and can be credited for moving gene therapy away from entire genes to precise changes in the genome. In 2017, it was the first company to perform gene editing in vivo in humans, with its mucopolysaccharidosis type 2 (Hunter syndrome) therapy SB-913.
